unit.subject: English
unit.unitOverview: In this unit, your child learns about their five senses — seeing, hearing, smelling, tasting and touching — and the body parts we use for each one. They learn describing words (soft, loud, sweet, cold) and start to compare things (a tree is tall, a building is taller). Most of all, they explore the world around them and learn to be curious and thankful for all the wonderful things they can sense.
unit.learningPath:
Begin with the guided teacher launch and learn a few words through pictures and play.
Listen to the Unit story and join in with repeated language.
Complete six language, speaking, early-writing and practical activities.
Meet your teacher three times each week for two weeks.
Finish the ten-question checkpoint and tell an adult what you can do.

readings.title: Amal at the Market
readings.theme: the senses, body parts, descriptions and comparison
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
One sunny morning, Amal went to the market with her grandmother, Selma.

The market was big and busy. “Come, Amal,” said Selma. “Use your five senses today!”

First, Amal used her eyes to see. She saw red tomatoes and yellow bananas. She saw green mangoes in a big basket. “I can see so many colours!” said Amal.

Next, Amal used her ears to hear. She heard a man calling, “Fresh fish! Fresh fish!” She heard goats and a baby crying. “It is very loud here!” she said.

Then Amal used her nose to smell. She smelled warm bread. She smelled sweet flowers. She smelled hot spices from a big pot. “Mmm! This smells so good!” said Amal.

A kind man named Ishmael gave Amal a piece of ripe mango. She used her tongue to taste it. “It is sweet and juicy!” she said. “Thank you, Ishmael!”

Last, Amal used her hands to touch. She touched a soft cloth. It felt soft. She touched a smooth stone. It felt hard and cold. “I like to touch things,” said Amal.

“Look, Grandmother!” said Amal. “This melon is big. But that melon is bigger!”

Selma smiled. “Well done, Amal. You are using all your senses.”

On the way home, Amal held her grandmother's hand. “I saw pretty colours. I heard loud sounds. I smelled sweet bread. I tasted juicy mango. And I touched soft cloth,” she said.

“Alhamdulillah,” said Selma. “We must be thankful for our wonderful senses.”

“Yes,” said Amal happily. “Thank you, Allah, for my eyes, my ears, my nose, my tongue, and my hands!”

readings.title: Talk about My Five Senses
readings.theme: the senses, body parts, descriptions and comparison
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript: Look, point and talk. I can see with my eyes. I can hear with my ears. It is ___. This is ___ than that. These are my ___. Which sense do I use? An adult reads each line while the learner points, repeats and acts.

readings.title: My Five Senses rhyme
readings.theme: the senses, body parts, descriptions and comparison
readings.setting: Home, online lesson or Grade 1 classroom
readings.passageScript:
“Two Little Eyes” — say it together
Two little eyes to see all around.  /  Two little ears to hear each sound.  /  One little nose to smell what's sweet.  /  One little mouth that likes to eat.
Point to your eyes, ears, nose and mouth as you say each line. Then ask: which sense is NOT in the poem? (Touch! Wiggle your fingers.)
“The Music Man” — sing and pretend to play
I am the music man. I come from down your way. And I can play. What can you play?  /  I can play the piano, the piano, the piano. I can play the piano, pia-piano.
Pretend to play each instrument as you sing — piano, drum, violin. This helps your child listen for different sounds.

grammar.title: Language pattern 1
grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: I can see with my eyes.
grammar.ruleAndExamples: I can see with my eyes.
grammar.commonMistake: A Grade 1 learner may answer with one word. Model the whole short pattern warmly and let the learner try again.
grammar.memoryTip: Say it, tap it, then build it with word cards.
grammar.practice: Complete and say three examples using: I can see with my eyes.

grammar.explanation: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: I can hear with my ears.

speaking.title: Speaking 1 - Listen and point
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: I can see with my eyes. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I can see with my eyes. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 2 - Repeat the model
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: I can hear with my ears. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern I can hear with my ears. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 3 - Ask and answer
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: It is ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern It is ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 4 - Picture talk
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: This is ___ than that. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern This is ___ than that. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 5 - Role-play
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: These are my ___. An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern These are my ___. Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

speaking.title: Speaking 6 - My confident turn
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Which sense do I use? An adult models once, the learner repeats, then changes one word using a picture or real object.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: With an adult present, practise the pattern Which sense do I use? Give warm feedback on one clear word and one next try.

writing.title: Writing 1 - Draw and tell
writing.promptAndInstructions: Draw one idea about the senses, body parts, descriptions and comparison. Tell an adult what it shows.
writing.modelText: I can see with my eyes.
writing.sentenceStarter: I can see with my eyes.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 2 - Trace
writing.promptAndInstructions: Trace two useful words, saying each sound or word as you trace.
writing.modelText: I can hear with my ears.
writing.sentenceStarter: I can hear with my ears.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 3 - Match and copy
writing.promptAndInstructions: Match three words to pictures, then copy one word.
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 4 - Label
writing.promptAndInstructions: Add two labels to your drawing or picture.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 5 - Complete a pattern
writing.promptAndInstructions: Copy and complete: These are my ___.
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

writing.title: Writing 6 - Create one sentence
writing.promptAndInstructions: Choose a picture and complete one short sentence using: Which sense do I use?
writing.expectedLength: One label, phrase or short sentence
writing.successCriteria: I said the idea first; formed or selected meaningful letters and words; used the model; checked my work with an adult
writing.support: Adult read-aloud, picture choices, tracing dots, movable word cards and oral rehearsal are allowed.
writing.extension: Add a second label or sentence and read it aloud.

activities.title: Activity 1

activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 2

activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 4

activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

activities.title: Activity 5

activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI with adult or teacher support

liveSessions.title: Meet the words
liveSessions.beforeSession: Bring crayons and one real object connected to the Unit.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min hello song; 7 min model and repeat; 8 min game or story; 7 min guided practice; 3 min celebrate and preview
liveSessions.afterSession: Show one word, action, drawing or sentence from My Five Senses to an adult.

liveSessions.title: Story and talk

teacherNotes.note: Use 15-20 minute independent blocks inside the lesson. Read every instruction aloud, model before expecting a response, and accept pointing, acting, drawing, tracing or speaking as valid evidence.
A teacher or caregiver remains present whenever the young learner uses recording or AI tutor features. The adult controls the device and protects the child's personal information.

see. see is one of the Unit words for the five senses.
hear. hear is one of the Unit words for the five senses.
smell. smell is one of the Unit words for the five senses.
taste. taste is one of the Unit words for the five senses.
touch. touch is one of the Unit words for the five senses.
I can see with my eyes.. The approved Unit pattern is: I can see with my eyes.
I can hear with my ears.. The approved Unit pattern is: I can hear with my ears.
It is ___.. The approved Unit pattern is: It is ___.
This is ___ than that.. The approved Unit pattern is: This is ___ than that.
These are my ___.. The approved Unit pattern is: These are my ___.

selfAssessment.statement: I can name the five senses: see, hear, smell, taste, touch.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can point to and name the body parts we use: eyes, ears, nose, tongue, hands.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can say which sense goes with which body part (“I see with my eyes”).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can use describing words: soft, hard, loud, quiet, sweet, cold.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can compare two things (tall / taller; sweet / sweeter).
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself

selfAssessment.statement: I can join in the “Two Little Eyes” poem and “The Music Man” song.
selfAssessment.scale: Not yet | With help | By myself
